title: The Burgomaster's Funeral & the Church
subtitle: The Village of Barovia · The Cemetery · The Church of the Morning Lord
opener: Ireena Kolyana was not what the letter had implied. The burgomaster's plea spoke of someone to be protected. The woman who met them at the mansion door was already protecting herself — composed, direct, and completely aware of the situation she was in. She accepted the group's help because she was not foolish enough to refuse it, not because she needed rescuing.

paragraphs: Ismark's request was straightforward: bury their father before the party left, then escort Ireena to Vallaki. The group discussed it. Not everyone was immediately certain. But the answer was yes.

sections.heading: What Ismark Carried
paragraphs: Before the burial, Ismark told them why Strahd wanted Ireena above anyone else. He said it without preamble and without comfort — the words of a man who had been carrying the weight of them for a long time and had grown tired of the carrying. Ireena bore the soul of Tatyana, Strahd's ancient beloved. The vampire had been losing her for centuries, watching her reincarnate and die, and he had not stopped wanting her back.

Ireena was not present for the conversation. Whether Ismark had told her directly, or whether she had pieced it together herself, was left unsaid. The group absorbed it in silence. There was nothing useful to say about it yet.

sections.heading: The Church — Before the Funeral
paragraphs: They entered the church of the Morning Lord before the burial service. Something was audible from below — a sound from the basement, something between an animal and a human voice, intermittent and wrong. They did not accept it. Several of them moved toward the door that led down, wanting to see. Father Donavich stepped in front of it. He did not explain. He did not ask. He simply stood there until they stopped.
The sounds continued through the preparations. No one mentioned them again.

sections.heading: The Burial
paragraphs: The cemetery was quiet in the way Barovia is always quiet — not peaceful, just empty of resistance. Father Donavich performed the rites with mechanical precision. He was present in body. Whatever animated him as a priest, as a man, was somewhere else entirely. The village sent almost no one.

sections.heading: Donavich Speaks
paragraphs: After the earth covered Kolyan Indirovich, the father finally talked. His son Doru had gone to Castle Ravenloft with a monster hunter, believing they could end Strahd. Doru came back changed — a vampire spawn, barely recognisable, chained in the church basement where his father had been keeping him for months, praying for a miracle that had not come. The monster hunter's name meant nothing to the group. What mattered was the result.

Donavich gave the group something useful alongside the grief: a clear account of vampire weaknesses, the tools that could hurt them, the conditions that could kill them. And one more thing — if Strahd were destroyed, Doru would be freed from his control. The spawn do not outlast their master.

Sarda wanted to go down. She said so clearly — the son was still in there somewhere, and leaving him in the dark felt like abandonment dressed up as practicality. The others were less certain. The debate was real, not comfortable. In the end, the door stayed shut. Doru remained a sound from below, nothing more. There was no version of opening that door that ended well tonight.

sections.heading: Sarda at the Church
paragraphs: Sarda felt it most — a father who could not release his son, a son who could not return. But her discomfort was not a cleric's crisis of faith. Sarda's juramento no depende de que el Señor de la Mañana responda. Su pacto es con la luz misma, con la vida, con la magia del mundo. Lo que la turbó fue otra cosa: que un lugar dedicado a preservar la luz llevara tanto tiempo apagado, y que nadie lo hubiera notado excepto el hombre al otro lado de esa puerta. Faith as a structure assumes the structure holds. In Barovia, the Morning Lord's church had been standing for a long time, but the Morning Lord had been very quiet — and Sarda, whose oath asks nothing of gods, found that silence harder to explain than she expected.

sections.heading: Bildrath's Mercantile
paragraphs: Before returning to Ismark's, the group passed through the village's only shop. Bildrath Cantemir received them with the specific warmth of a man who has no competition and knows it — cooperative in manner, useless in substance. His nephew Parriwimple stood nearby, large and quiet in the way that serves as a statement. The shop's inventory matched its owner: an oil lamp and a frayed cloak were among the offerings. The group left without buying anything.
Back at the mansion, Ireena had a bag ready. Kolyan was in the ground. Vallaki was on the map. The route existed. Strahd knew where they were.
The road would not be quiet.

known: Ireena carries the soul of Tatyana, Strahd's ancient beloved — this is why he wants her
Doru followed a monster hunter to Ravenloft and returned as a vampire spawn
If Strahd dies, Doru is freed from his control
Vampire weaknesses: sunlight, running water, holy symbols, stakes
Ireena is ready and willing to leave for Vallaki
Bildrath runs the only shop in the village — Parriwimple is his nephew and enforcer
unanswered: Who was the monster hunter who led Doru to Ravenloft — and where are they now?
Does Ireena know what she carries? Has she accepted it?
What awaits on the road to Vallaki?
people: Ireena Kolyana — composed, capable, bearer of Tatyana's soul
Ismark — Ireena's brother, exhausted and ashamed of what he had to say
Father Donavich — broken but informative, guardian of a door he will not open
Doru — chained in the church basement, heard but never seen
Bildrath Cantemir — village shopkeeper, cooperative and useless
Parriwimple — Bildrath's nephew, large, quiet, present
sidebar.milestone: Kolyan Indirovich is buried. The group has committed to escorting Ireena to Vallaki.
